steps.title: Convert the time
steps.content: The population increases by $p\%$ every $240$ seconds. Since $240$ seconds is $4$ minutes, compare $N(m+4)$ with $N(m)$.

steps.title: Find the growth factor
steps.content:
Use the ratio of the two population values.
$$\frac{N(m+4)}{N(m)}=\frac{72Q^{(m+4)/8}}{72Q^{m/8}}=Q^{(m+4)/8-m/8}=Q^{1/2}$$

steps.title: Convert to percent increase
steps.content:
A $p\%$ increase has growth factor $1+\frac{p}{100}$.
$$1+\frac{p}{100}=Q^{1/2}$$
$$p=100(Q^{1/2}-1)$$
The answer is C.
